question.content: For the beam with supports at A, B, and C under end couple M_0, solve the three vertical reactions A_y, B_y, and C_y. Output exactly one tuple token in the order (A_y,B_y,C_y), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: (A_y,B_y,C_y).

step_1.content: Set the beam with points A, B, C, lengths AB=a and BC=a, and external end couple M_0.
step_1.key_point: Unknown reactions are A_y, B_y, C_y.

step_3.content: Use global equilibrium and the compatibility relation from the second-integration method for segment continuity.
step_3.key_point: From BC-segment integration, B_y and A_y are linked.

step_5.content: Cut segment AB at D: write M(x), then integrate to theta and v, applying v_B=0 and continuity at B.
step_5.key_point: AB gives one compatibility equation with A_y.

step_7.content: Cut segment BC at H and combine with equilibrium equations to solve all reactions.
step_7.key_point: Obtain A_y, B_y, C_y as functions of M_0 and a.
